what_it_does: This file is a JSON interpretive ledger entry that records the findings of a mid-level-of-detail redundancy analysis of two source folders (company-info and presentation-15p) for the claude-ds project, stating that the analysis confirms the existing design model with no new grammar and refines the diagram generator rule by treating the tangle-to-hub morph as a deterministic, animatable graph transform.
observations: Header marks these as the 6th-7th folders and a 'mid-LOD redundancy check'; verdict is 'fully confirms the model; no new grammar' plus one diagram refinement.

Confirms the same DNA at 16:9 across now 7 consistent folders: zoning ladder, gold->bronze ramp, frame signature (V mark, hatch), triangle/arrow bullets, blueprint ghost, soft panels, thin icons, octagon/hex Vi-Hub badge.
States mid-LOD sits exactly between terse (deck1-2026) and high-detail (recent-pitches), as the LOD axis predicts.

The starred refinement: presentation-15p p-06 & p-11 and company-info show the same hub diagram in two states side by side with an arrow between them — a fully-connected tangle morphing to the same nodes routed through a central hub.

Generalises the morph to a rule: 'same node-set, re-route edges through a hub' is a deterministic transform on a {nodes, edges} graph, the output the diagram generator (D4) should produce, and inherently animatable.

Logs minor confirmations: octagon Vi-Hub / hexagon Property-Wizard shape-coded badges (shape=semantic), a flow-stepper atom with a + join node, map locator + big-stat hatch footer, file-extension data-exhaust list.
Diff section concludes '7 folders consistent — model is comprehensively validated' and refines D4 (diagram = {nodes,edges} + state).
Closes 'Corpus complete' — all 12 source folders analysed — and signals readiness to move from analysis to synthesis starting with token recalibration.
Contains self-aware concerns: it calls itself a 'strategic sample' rather than a full page-by-page pass, and notes that roll-up tallies rely on sibling docs and PROGRESS.md.
standouts: The chaos->order morph reframed from a recurring drawing into a deterministic graph transform (re-route N-by-N edges through a hub) that is both generator output and a prime motion component.
diagram = {nodes, edges} + state — promoting 'state' to a first-class input so diagrams are generated from a spec+state, not authored.
shape=semantic: octagon vs hexagon badges encode meaning, not decoration.
conventions: evidence-diff discipline: confirms / refines (pencil) / new (plus) / no-contradictions markers.
axis=f(content,position): LOD treated as a predictive axis (mid sits between terse and high-detail).
generated-not-authored: diagrams produced from a {nodes,edges}+state spec.
redundancy-check-as-validation: a folder that adds no new grammar is a positive confirmation, not a waste.
concerns: Describes itself as a 'strategic sample' of the two folders rather than a full page-by-page pass, so the 'fully confirms' verdict rests on sampled evidence.
The 7-folders-consistent and 12/12-complete tallies are roll-up claims whose per-folder evidence lives in sibling docs and PROGRESS.md, not provable from this file alone.
notes: Mid-LOD confirmation ledger entry that adds no new grammar but extracts one generator rule: the tangle-to-hub diagram morph is a deterministic, animatable graph transform produced from a {nodes,edges,state} spec.
questions: Was the 'state-morph' shown as two literal frames + arrow in the source, or is the deterministic-transform reading the analyst's inference about how it should be generated?
Which folders make up the '7 folders consistent' count, and is each genuinely full-pass or also sampled?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it is framed as a mid-LOD redundancy/confirmation check and it delivers a confirmation plus the single promised diagram refinement, then declares the corpus complete.
intention_signals: Inferred: the analysis is trying to validate the model by redundancy (mid-LOD should add nothing, and it doesn't) while mining the one genuinely new structural insight — that the recurring before/after hub visual is a deterministic, animatable graph transform — so the diagram generator and motion layer can produce it from a spec rather than redraw it.
novelty: Elevates a recurring 'before/after' marketing visual into a formal, deterministic, animatable graph operation (re-route edges through a hub) and names diagram-state as a first-class generator input.
contribution: Contributes the D4 refinement (diagram = {nodes,edges} + state with chaos->order as a deterministic animatable transform), the shape=semantic badge atom, the flow-stepper-with-join variant, and the validation that mid-LOD adds no new grammar — all of which a fused system would keep.
summary_for_embedding: Mid-LOD redundancy analysis of two mid-detail deck folders confirming the design model across seven consistent folders with no new grammar, refining the diagram finding so a diagram is {nodes,edges}+state and the tangle-to-hub morph is a deterministic, animatable graph transform.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to validate the generative model by redundancy at mid LOD while extracting the one new rule — that the recurring chaos-to-order hub visual is a deterministic, animatable graph transform the generator and motion layer should produce from a spec rather than authored by hand.
